description: §23.5.2 — a capability token MAY repeat with additional args; the args UNION. `capabilities=[network("a.com"), network("b.com")]` is the SPEC's own worked equivalence (≡ `network` allowing `{a.com, b.com}`). It COMPILES CLEAN: the repeated `network` is NOT a duplicate-attribute error, NOT an unknown token, and the two hosts merge into one allow-list rather than the second shadowing the first. Complements `all-tokens-valid` (each token once) by exercising the repeat-and-union path specifically.
rationale: §23.5.2: a capability token MAY repeat with additional args; the args UNION (`network("a.com"), network("b.com")` ≡ `network` allowing `{a.com, b.com}`). The special-parsed capability-token list accepts the repeat with no diagnostic. Only the incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED remains; E-FOREIGN-CAPABILITY-UNKNOWN, E-SCOPE-001, and W-ATTR-001 all stay silent.
